title: Exit Methods Should Not be Called in Java
description: Exit methods such as System.exit(int status), Rutime.getRuntime().exit(int status), and Runtime.getRuntime().halt(int) should not be called as they shut down the entire Java virtual machine. These methods should only be used with extreme care, and only when the intent is to stop the whole Java process. In a J2EE container, these methods should never be used.
recommendation: Avoid using exit methods unless necessary. Make sure that you understand the implications of using such methods in your code.
impact: Using exit methods can lead to abrupt termination of the Java virtual machine, potentially causing data loss or other unexpected behavior.

checks.message: Avoid using exit methods unless necessary.
